title: Near-memory Dequantization Architecture In Custom HBM for LLM inference (SK hynix)
excerpt: Researchers from SK hynix published a technical paper titled “StreamDQ: Near-Memory Weight DeQuantization in Custom HBM for Scalable AI Inference Acceleration.” The paper proposes StreamDQ for “a lightweight architectural enhancement that enables on-the-fly dequantization in the memory subsystem for high-throughput, large-batch LLM inference,” and reports “up to 7.08× speedup and 90.23% lower energy” for mixed-precision… » read more

title: Co-Packaged Optics for Multi-Die Designs
excerpt: CPO reduces electrical loss, improves energy efficiency and reach, and enables bandwidth densities that are increasingly difficult to achieve with copper alone.

title: Can Fine-Pitch Hybrid Bonding Go High Volume?
